commands.repair_reason: - [P2] Maintainer/security review should decide the malware-threshold policy, Resend supply-chain/env rollout, and dirty merge refresh before merge.; Needs attention: The diff has a concrete supply-chain and security-workflow concern: it adds Resend as the production email provider while Socket reports a publisher-change warning.; Review finding: [P3] Make plugin escalation copy artifact-aware — convex/lib/emails.ts... (sha=3b4cc2d9806ed48b8e3435c84494eea4691c2448)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds Resend-backed moderation emails, artifact-level malicious upload quarantine with thresholded account autobans, and a dedicated banned-account appeal route with docs/spec/test coverage.

Reproducibility: not applicable. as a feature/security-policy PR rather than a bug report. The provided proof screenshots and listed tests exercise the new rendered emails and banned-account route.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] Maintainer/security review should decide the malware-threshold policy, Resend supply-chain/env rollout, and dirty merge refresh before merge.

target.title: feat: improve account appeal notifications
target.body: ## Summary This PR turns scanner-triggered malware handling into a coherent two-level flow: - artifact-level scanner rejections block that specific skill/plugin version and email the publisher remediation guidance - existing clean latest versions stay live; malicious first versions/releases do not publish a public latest - account-level bans and Hermit appeals stay reserved for account-level enforcement after repeated malicious rejections - banned-account sign-in errors now route to a dedicated appeal page instead of looking like generic login trouble - Resend-backed account emails cover bans, restores, and malicious-artifact warnings ## User-Facing Proof Proof comment with uploaded artifacts: https://github.com/openclaw/clawhub/pull/2520#issuecomment-4636295683 ### Rendered Emails Rendered from the real `convex/lib/emails.ts` builders. Left is the account-level ban email with only high-level account impact plus the Hermit appeal link. Right is the artifact-level scanner rejection email, which keeps local `clawhub scan` guidance and the repeated-rejection warning. ### Banned Sign-In Page Captured from a real running local ClawHub production preview, not an HTML mock. The proof navigated to `/dashboard?error_description=Account%20banned`; the app redirected to `/account-banned`, removed the red inline auth error state, and showed the appeal-only page. ## Flow Diagrams ### Artifact-Level Scanner Rejection ```mermaid flowchart TD A[Publisher uploads skill/plugin version] --> B[ClawHub security scan] B -->|clean or non-blocking| C[Promote version normally] B -->|malicious| D[Block candidate version/release] D --> E{Existing clean latest?} E -->|yes| F[Keep previous clean latest public] E -->|no| G[No public latest is promoted] D --> H[Email publisher: artifact blocked, account still active] H --> I[Publisher runs clawhub scan on fixed local copy] I --> J[Publisher uploads fixed version] D --> K{Escalation threshold crossed?} K -->|no| L[No account appeal path] K -->|yes| M[Account-level autoban] ``` ### Account-Level Ban + Hermit Appeal ```mermaid flowchart TD A[Account is banned: manual, rate-limit, or repeated malicious rejections] --> B[ClawHub sends account ban email] A --> C[Banned sign-in attempt] B --> D[Appeal link: appeals.openclaw.ai] C --> E[Dedicated /account-banned page] E --> D D --> F[Hermit appeal tool handles account-level review] F -->|accepted| G[ClawHub appeal service unbans/restores eligible listings] F -->|rejected| H[Account remains banned] ``` ## Behavioral Notes - Artifact warning emails intentionally do not include an appeal link. - The artifact email tells users their account can still sign in, the rejected version was not made public, they can upload a fixed version, and: “Repeated malicious rejections may lead to account disablement.” - Account ban emails include the affected skill/plugin when known, a high-level reason, and the Hermit appeal link. - Account ban emails no longer include local scan-result guidance; that copy stays on skill/plugin flagging emails only. - Rate-limit/manual bans do not get scanner remediation copy. - Generic `Forbidden` permission errors still render as permission errors and do not route to `/account-banned`. - `/account-banned` is reserved as a route slug so it cannot be claimed by a skill slug. ## Tests / Verification Latest head `3b4cc2d9`: - `bun run format:check -- convex/lib/skillSlugValidator.ts convex/lib/skillSlugValidator.test.ts src/lib/authErrorMessage.ts src/lib/authErrorMessage.test.ts src/components/SignInPrompt.tsx src/components/SignInPrompt.test.tsx src/routes/__root.tsx src/routes/account-banned.tsx src/routes/-account-banned.test.tsx src/components/AppProviders.tsx src/components/AppProviders.test.tsx src/components/SignInButton.tsx src/components/SignInButton.test.tsx src/components/Header.tsx src/components/SkillDetailPage.tsx convex/lib/emails.ts convex/lib/emails.test.ts` — passed - `bunx vitest run convex/lib/emails.test.ts convex/lib/skillSlugValidator.test.ts src/components/AppProviders.test.tsx src/routes/-account-banned.test.tsx src/lib/authErrorMessage.test.ts src/components/SignInButton.test.tsx src/components/SignInPrompt.test.tsx` — 90 tests passed - `bunx tsc --noEmit --pretty false` — passed - `VITE_CONVEX_URL=https://example.invalid bun run build` — passed - Real preview/browser proof: `http://127.0.0.1:4179/dashboard?error_description=Account%20banned` redirected to `/account-banned`; screenshot uploaded above - Remote proof hashes verified after publish: - banned sign-in page: `9d51a2dfb1032597a99ca3eda6417e0bd20f4fe8a55817e025193d68c1ccc26d` - rendered emails: `698efa2d81f9ea574f25cf621cb3259e6dc8c4b7b41ac365c45e5f596e142a67` - `AUTOREVIEW_AUTO_TESTS=0 .agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local` — `autoreview clean: no accepted/actionable findings reported` Earlier branch verification before the final banned-page/email-copy adjustment: - `bun run ci:static` — passed - `bun run ci:unit` — passed - `bunx vitest run convex/lib/emails.test.ts convex/users.test.ts convex/skills.rateLimit.test.ts convex/packages.public.test.ts` — passed - `PLAYWRIGHT_LOCAL_AUTH_CONVEX_URL=http://127.0.0.1:3310 PLAYWRIGHT_LOCAL_AUTH_CONVEX_SITE_URL=http://127.0.0.1:3311 PLAYWRIGHT_PORT=4176 bun run test:pw:local-auth -- --project=chromium e2e/pr-2520-banned-auth-proof.pw.test.ts` — passed locally with a temporary proof test; captured the live auth screenshot, then removed the temp test from the branch
